content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 3,
1992
Laura S.
Seo
Deputy City Clerk
4455 West 126th Street
Hawthorne,
CA
90250
RE:
Combined Campaign Report
Our File No.
A—92-400
Dear Ms.
Seo:
This is to confirm advice provided to your office on July 22,
1992,
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).l/
Section 84205 states:
The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required
to be filed by this title.
In order to ensure that ballot measure petitions intended for
the November 3,
1992,
election were certified in time for the
measure to appear on that ballot,
the City of Hawthorne imposed
June 26,
1992,
as the administrative deadline for filing
petitions,
which is earlier than the 150-day deadline imposed by
the Elections Code.
(Elections Code Section 3515.)
The deadline
for filing petitions imposed by the Elections Code is December 17,
1992,
which falls after the election date.
The Hawthorne Economic
Improvement Committee was successful in its qualification effort
and will be required to file a semi-annual statement through June
30,
1992,
due July 31,
1992.
The campaign statement due in
connection with the qualification effort
(the
21—day statement),
when filed in connection with the filing deadlines imposed by the
November 3rd election,
would normally cover the period October 18,
1992 through December
1,
1992,
with a filing deadline of December
8,
1992.
(Section 84200.5.)
However, the Commission believes
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


Laura S.
Seo
Our File No.
A-92-400
Page
2
that it is appropriate for the committee to combine this report
with the semi—annual statement covering the period October 18,
1992 through December 31,
1992,
due January 31,
1993.
If you have any questions regarding the above,
please do not
hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

sections.conclusion: The Commission believes that it is appropriate for the committee to combine this report with the semi-annual statement covering the period October 18, 1992 through December 31, 1992, due January 31, 1993.
sections.question_synthetic: May the Hawthorne Economic Improvement Committee combine its 21-day campaign statement (due in connection with the November 3, 1992 election) with its semi-annual statement covering October 18 through December 31, 1992?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC approves combining the 21-day campaign statement with the semi-annual statement, allowing the committee to file a single combined report due January 31, 1993 covering both periods.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a confirmed advice letter following July 22, 1992 oral advice. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with standard headers, but question and conclusion are clearly discernible from the letter text. Technical discussion of statutory compliance under Government Code Section 84205 and Elections Code Section 3515.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May the Hawthorne Economic Improvement Committee combine its 21-day campaign statement (due in connection with the November 3, 1992 election) with its semi-annual statement covering October 18 through December 31, 1992?

CONCLUSION: The Commission believes that it is appropriate for the committee to combine this report with the semi-annual statement covering the period October 18, 1992 through December 31, 1992, due January 31, 1993.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission August 3, 1992 Laura S. Seo Deputy City Clerk 4455 West 126th Street Hawthorne, CA 90250 RE: Combined Campaign Report Our File No. A—92-400 Dear Ms. Seo: This is to confirm advice provided to your office on July 22, 1992, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).l/ Section 84205 states: The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title. In order to ensure that ballot measure petitions intended for the November 3, 1992, election were certified in time for the measure to appear on that ballot, the City of Hawthorne imposed June 26, 1992, as the administrative deadline for filing petitions, which is earlier than the 150-day deadline imposed by the Elections Code. (Elections Code Section 3515.) The deadline for filing petitions imposed by the Elections Code is December 17, 1992, which falls after the election date. The Hawthorne Economic Improvement Committee was successful in its qualification effort and will be required to file a semi-annual statement through June 30, 1992, due July 31, 1992. The campaign statement due in connection with the qualification effort (the 21—day statement), when filed in connection with the filing deadlines imposed by the November 3rd election, would normally cover the period October 18, 1992 through December 1, 1992, with a filing deadline of December 8, 1992. (Section 84200.5.) However, the Commission believes 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Laura S. Seo Our File No. A-92-400 Page 2 that it is appropriate for the committee to combine this report with the semi—annual statement covering the period October 18, 1992 through December 31, 1992, due January 31, 1993. If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Mary Ann Kvasager Political Reform Consultant
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming authorization under Government Code Section 84205 for the Hawthorne Economic Improvement Committee to file a combined campaign report merging the 21-day qualification statement with the semi-annual statement due to timing complications from the city's June 26, 1992 petition deadline.
